name: Cova des Coloms ('the Cathedral') via the Barranc de Binigaus
description: One of the most impressive caves in Menorca, nicknamed 'the Cathedral' for its temple-like proportions (a 50 m central chamber, 24 m high), at the end of a level path through a green ravine. An outing with a touch of magic, fit for the whole family.

highlights: walk the leafy Barranc de Binigaus from Es Migjorn Gran, with almost no climb
enter through a discreet opening into a great chamber with a soaring ceiling (a possible prehistoric sanctuary)
extend the walk to the unspoilt beach of Binigaus
